mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing the present value of equity and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with federal funds rate declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, it signals severe labor market slack and impending corporate earnings deterioration, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which creates short-term positive momentum in equity markets during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months following positive INDPRO surprises, or the mechanism shows zero predictive alpha during recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL is followed by positive 20-day equity returns in over 65% of observations, or the negative correlation coefficient is statistically insignificant with a p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth falls below 100,000 per month, it indicates insufficient labor demand to support wage growth and consumer spending, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The threshold signal generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered months, or walk-forward Sharpe ratio remains below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth consistently outpaces consumer price inflation, household purchasing power expands, driving higher corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression, or 20-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of periods where real growth exceeds inflation.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound environment with quantitative easing, persistently low federal funds rates eliminate term premium and force portfolio rebalancing into equities, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive bias when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, or the signal fails to generate excess returns relative to a risk-free benchmark during QE periods.
